title: NOC 22311 – Electronic Service Technicians (Household and Business Equipment) | GTR Immigration
description: Electronic Service Technicians (Household and Business Equipment) are in strong demand across Canada, driven by rising consumer-electronics usage, office-automation needs, retail-service expansion, and post-pandemic equipment upgrades across businesses and homes nationwide. Classified under NOC 22311 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Install, maintain & repair household and office electronics
Test circuits using multimeters, oscilloscopes & logic probes
Adjust and align systems using manuals & schematics
employment_requirements: Two- to three-year college program in electronics or
Four-year apprenticeship in electronic servicing or
Trade certification (voluntary) in Ontario, British Columbia & Yukon
job_outlook: This favourable gap makes the occupation attractive for candidates with:
pathways: Strong demand in retail & corporate services
Advancement into supervisory or technical-specialist roles
additional_information: Canadian employers actively recruit electronic-service professionals for:
Consumer-electronics retailersOffice-equipment service firmsTelecom & satellite providersElectronics manufacturersRepair & maintenance contractorsField-service departments
Annual salaries typically range between $31,800–$77,000 CAD, depending on province, certification, and service specialization.
